zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡偏强整理
zh.content:
整体处于高位震荡上行结构，价格稳站71美元上方，短线维持温和多头，但动能略有放缓，进入整理阶段

技术指标显示均线系统支撑明显，价格运行在SMA20（69.59）之上，MACD维持正值，市场偏多但未放量

下方支撑关注69.5与68.8区域，上方阻力在71.56前高及72.5附近，突破需成交量配合确认

操作建议以回调低吸为主，回踩70-69.6分批布局，多头止损设69下方，目标看72至73区间

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Solana against the US dollar is currently trading in a narrow consolidation phase around 71.37 after a modest 0.20% daily gain. Price action remains tightly compressed between 71.12 and 71.56, indicating equilibrium between buyers and sellers. The structure remains mildly constructive as price holds above the 20-day simple moving average at 69.589, suggesting that the short-term trend has shifted from recovery into early-stage consolidation with a bullish bias.

Momentum indicators are mixed but still lean positive. The MACD reading at 0.8824 indicates that upside momentum has not fully dissipated, although the absence of a visible signal line limits confirmation of trend strength. With RSI unavailable, price behavior becomes more important, and the tight range suggests cooling volatility rather than distribution. Overall momentum appears stable rather than accelerating, consistent with consolidation after a recent advance.

Immediate support is found near the 20-day SMA at 69.6, which also acts as the key dynamic level defining bullish structure. A break below this area could expose downside pressure toward 68.5 and potentially the mid-67 zone. On the upside, resistance is concentrated at 71.56, the recent high, followed by a stronger barrier near 73.0, where previous supply zones may re-emerge and cap further advances.

Trading strategy favors range-bound tactics until a breakout occurs. Buying dips toward 69.6–70.0 with protective stops below the moving average offers a favorable risk structure. Alternatively, a breakout above 71.6 with volume expansion could trigger momentum continuation toward 73.0 and beyond. Until either boundary is decisively broken, traders may benefit from mean-reversion approaches while monitoring volatility contraction for expansion signals.
